content_of_post_to_comment:
So I have an idea to stop phone addiction. Get the most embarrassing phone case possible, so you never want to take your phone out in public.

Why I think it might work: Most people still need to carry their phone with them for emergencies or practical reasons, but they want to reduce the urge to use it unnecessarily when they're out. An embarrassing phone case could make you think twice before pulling it out unless you actually need to.

If you get a really embarrassing phone case, you might even start finding alternatives to things you'd normally do on your phone: using your bank card instead of Apple Pay, reading a book instead of checking Instagram, or just waiting until you get home.

I don't know if this is actually a good idea cause it would not work in private, only in public, but I think I'm going to try it. Let me know what you think.
comment: Feels like adding social friction to create a pause before pulling the phone out, which might work at first, but I wonder if people just get used to the case and the effect fades. Also in private the habit is unchanged like you said. I’ve had more luck with making apps harder to reach (logouts, grayscale) than relying on embarrassment since the urge is mostly automatic, not social.
